user_request: Design a comprehensive year-long Algebra I curriculum for 9th-grade students that integrates real-world problem solving, scaffolded instruction, and formative assessment checkpoints aligned with Common Core State Standards. Include unit sequencing, pacing guides, and cross-curricular connections to science and financial literacy.
user_goal: Obtain a detailed, standards-aligned curriculum framework that can be directly adopted by a math department for the upcoming school year

excellent_characteristics: Full CCSS alignment with specific standard codes cited
Detailed weekly pacing across 36 weeks
Meaningful cross-curricular connections with concrete examples
Built-in remediation and acceleration pathways

common_failures: Missing specific standard codes
Unrealistic pacing that doesn't account for review days
Generic cross-curricular connections without actionable examples
No differentiation for diverse learners
edge_cases: Students entering without pre-algebra mastery
Schools on block vs. traditional schedules
Classrooms with 1:1 device access vs. no technology

user_request: Create a 45-minute lesson plan for teaching 3rd-grade students how to identify the main idea and supporting details in a nonfiction passage about animals. Include a warm-up activity, guided practice, independent work, and a closing reflection.
user_goal: Get a ready-to-use lesson plan that a new teacher can follow step by step for a single class period

excellent_characteristics: Precise time allocations totaling 45 minutes
Age-appropriate vocabulary and activities
Clear teacher script/talking points
Built-in formative check for understanding
minimum_requirements: Related to main idea and supporting details

common_failures: Time allocations exceeding 45 minutes
Activities too advanced for 3rd graders
Missing transitions between segments
edge_cases: English Language Learners in the classroom
Students who finish early

user_request: Develop a comprehensive rubric and peer-assessment checklist for evaluating 10th-grade biology lab reports on enzyme activity experiments. The rubric should cover scientific methodology, data analysis, written communication, and proper citation of sources, using a 4-point scale with detailed descriptors for each level.
user_goal: Obtain a standardized assessment tool that ensures consistent and fair grading across multiple class sections taught by different teachers

excellent_characteristics: All four domains covered with distinct 4-point descriptors
Student-friendly peer-assessment checklist aligned with rubric
Observable and measurable criteria at each level
Calibration examples for borderline cases
minimum_requirements: 4-point rubric covering at least three domains

common_failures: Vague descriptors like 'good' or 'adequate' without specifics
Missing peer-assessment checklist
Inconsistent granularity across rubric dimensions
No distinction between content and communication quality
edge_cases: Lab reports with excellent data but poor writing
Students who collaborated but submitted individually
Reports using unconventional but valid methodologies

user_request: Design a complete 6-module asynchronous e-learning course on Data Privacy and GDPR Compliance for non-technical employees at a multinational corporation. Each module should include learning objectives, interactive scenario-based activities, knowledge checks, multimedia content descriptions, and estimated completion times. The course must accommodate employees across EU, US, and APAC regions with varying regulatory contexts.
user_goal: Produce a full instructional design document that an e-learning development team can use to build the course in an LMS like Articulate Rise or Moodle

excellent_characteristics: Six fully detailed modules with 3-5 learning objectives each
Interactive branching scenarios reflecting real workplace situations
Region-specific regulatory content for EU, US, and APAC
Accessibility considerations including WCAG 2.1 compliance notes
acceptable_characteristics: Six modules with clear learning objectives
At least one scenario per module
minimum_requirements: Six distinct modules on GDPR topics
Some form of knowledge assessment per module

common_failures: EU-centric content ignoring US and APAC regulations
Generic scenarios not grounded in realistic workplace situations
Missing accessibility considerations
No clear progression or prerequisite logic between modules
edge_cases: Employees in countries with no specific data privacy legislation
Managers who need deeper compliance knowledge than general staff
Employees with limited English proficiency

user_request: Design a classroom gamification system for a 7th-grade World History unit on Ancient Civilizations (Mesopotamia, Egypt, Greece, Rome) that spans 4 weeks. Include a point system, achievement badges, team-based quests, a narrative wrapper, and a leaderboard structure that motivates without discouraging lower-performing students.
user_goal: Create an engaging gamification framework that increases student participation and content retention while maintaining an inclusive classroom environment

excellent_characteristics: Coherent narrative arc tying all four civilizations together
Multiple pathways to earn points beyond just test scores
Anti-discouragement mechanics like personal-best tracking
Clear weekly progression with escalating challenges
acceptable_characteristics: Logical connection between game elements and content
Point system with varied earning opportunities

common_failures: Gamification that rewards speed over understanding
Leaderboards that embarrass struggling students
Game mechanics disconnected from learning objectives
Overly complex systems that confuse middle schoolers
edge_cases: Students who refuse to participate in competitive activities
Uneven team sizes due to absences

user_request: Develop a comprehensive Individualized Education Program (IEP) goal bank and accommodation framework for students with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) in grades K-5, covering communication, social skills, executive functioning, sensory regulation, and academic access. Include measurable annual goals with short-term benchmarks, evidence-based intervention strategies, data collection methods, and a decision tree for selecting appropriate accommodations based on student profiles.
user_goal: Provide special education teachers and IEP teams with a research-backed, practical resource that streamlines IEP development while ensuring individualized and legally compliant goal-setting

excellent_characteristics: SMART goals across all five domains with grade-band specificity
Evidence-based strategies citing current research like ABA, TEACCH, or Social Thinking
Decision tree with clear branching logic based on functional assessments
Data collection templates with frequency and duration tracking
acceptable_characteristics: Measurable goals in at least four domains
Some form of accommodation selection guidance
minimum_requirements: Goals covering communication and social skills at minimum

common_failures: Non-measurable goals using vague language like 'will improve'
Generic accommodations not specific to ASD
Ignoring sensory and executive functioning domains
No connection between assessment data and goal selection
edge_cases: Nonverbal students requiring AAC-integrated goals
Twice-exceptional students who are gifted and have ASD
Students transitioning from self-contained to inclusive settings

user_request: Create five hands-on STEM activities for 4th-grade students exploring the water cycle. Each activity should use inexpensive, easily available materials, take 30-45 minutes to complete, include step-by-step instructions, discussion questions, and a connection to real-world weather phenomena.
user_goal: Provide a classroom-ready set of engaging, low-cost STEM activities that reinforce water cycle concepts through experiential learning

excellent_characteristics: Five distinct activities each targeting a different water cycle stage
Materials costing under $5 per activity
Clear safety notes where applicable
Extension questions linking to climate and weather
acceptable_characteristics: Five activities related to the water cycle
At least one discussion question each
minimum_requirements: Includes materials lists and basic instructions

common_failures: Activities requiring expensive lab equipment
Instructions too complex for 4th graders to follow
Missing safety precautions for activities involving heat
edge_cases: Classrooms without sink access
Students with sensory sensitivities to water or wet materials

user_request: Generate a complete midterm examination for an undergraduate Data Structures and Algorithms course covering arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, and basic sorting algorithms. Include 20 multiple-choice questions, 5 short-answer questions, and 3 coding problems. Provide an answer key with detailed explanations and a Bloom's taxonomy classification for each question.
user_goal: Create a balanced, rigorous midterm exam that accurately assesses student understanding across multiple cognitive levels and can be administered in a 90-minute session

excellent_characteristics: Even topic distribution across all six data structure topics
Bloom's taxonomy levels ranging from Remember to Create
Plausible distractors in MCQs that reflect common misconceptions
Coding problems with sample inputs, outputs, and time complexity requirements
acceptable_characteristics: All topics covered with minor imbalance
At least three Bloom's levels represented
minimum_requirements: Covers at least five of six topics

common_failures: MCQ distractors that are obviously wrong
Coding problems with ambiguous specifications
Over-concentration on one topic
Missing time complexity expectations in coding problems
edge_cases: Students who may use different programming languages
Questions that could have multiple valid approaches

user_request: Design a 6-week interdisciplinary project-based learning unit where 11th-grade students investigate local water quality issues, collect and analyze real environmental data, collaborate with community stakeholders, and present policy recommendations to a public audience. Include driving questions, milestones, scaffolded research protocols, collaboration guidelines, a differentiated assessment plan, and connections to AP Environmental Science standards.
user_goal: Deliver a comprehensive PBL blueprint that guides teachers through facilitating an authentic, community-connected learning experience with rigorous academic standards

excellent_characteristics: Compelling driving question with authentic community relevance
Weekly milestones with clear deliverables and formative checks
Research protocols that teach scientific data collection methods
Public presentation component with stakeholder engagement plan

common_failures: Driving questions that are too broad or too narrow
Missing scaffolding for students unfamiliar with field research
No plan for community partner engagement logistics
Assessment focused only on final product with no process evaluation
edge_cases: Schools in urban areas without accessible natural water sources
Community partners who become unavailable mid-project
Students with mobility limitations affecting field work

user_request: Create a comprehensive academic advising decision framework for university advisors working with undeclared sophomore students at risk of not graduating within four years. The framework should include a structured intake assessment protocol, a decision matrix for recommending major exploration pathways based on student interests, aptitudes, and labor market data, intervention triggers for at-risk indicators (low GPA, excessive credit hours without declaration, disengagement), sample advising conversation scripts, and a semester-by-semester recovery planning template that accounts for prerequisite chains and credit-hour limits.
user_goal: Equip academic advisors with a systematic, data-informed tool that improves time-to-degree outcomes and student satisfaction for the most vulnerable undeclared student population

excellent_characteristics: Intake assessment covering academic, psychosocial, and career dimensions
Decision matrix integrating Holland codes or similar with BLS labor market projections
Tiered intervention triggers with specific advisor action items
Conversation scripts using motivational interviewing techniques
acceptable_characteristics: Major recommendation guidance based on student factors

common_failures: Overly prescriptive framework that ignores student agency
Missing labor market or outcomes data integration
Generic conversation scripts without motivational interviewing principles
Recovery plans that don't account for prerequisite dependencies
edge_cases: Transfer students with complex credit evaluations
Students considering interdisciplinary or self-designed majors
First-generation students unfamiliar with academic advising norms
